title: muhlenbergia brachyphylla
intro: Muhlenbergia brachyphylla, also known as the Mexican hair grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Muhlenbergia brachyphylla include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often made into teas, tinctures, or infused in oil for topical application or internal use.
